sample.title: Inside the Race to Build AI Data Centers in Space
sample.description:
“Data centers in space” may sound like science fiction, but as the SpaceX IPO augurs a space boom, tech giants and startups are betting orbital artificial intelligence is the next frontier.

How to Build a Data Center in Space: https://bloom.bg/4fSeMXn
Elon Musk and Jeff Bezos plan to make AI extraterrestrial.

Building data centers in space is still a relatively nascent idea, but it’s gaining traction and financial backing, as energy and space constraints limit the expansion of massive computing facilities here on Earth. Earlier this year, in the latest leg of their decades-long space race, Elon Musk’s SpaceX and Jeff Bezos’ Blue Origin both announced plans to build and launch so-called orbital data centers.

Learn more: https://bloom.bg/4fSeMXn

00:00-01:36 The AI data center squeeze
01:37-03:43 Starcloud’s big ambition
03:44-04:17 Why data centers in space make sense
04:18-8:15 The engineering challenges and possible solutions
08:16-09:28 Reducing the cost of launch
09:29-09:57 Global race to get there 
09:58-11:47 China’s different approach
11:48-13:21 Who will control the next internet? 

--------

sample.title: Dario Amodei Addresses Reports of Claude’s Role in US Military Strikes
sample.description:
On The Circuit with Emily Chang, Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei addresses reports that the US military is using Claude as part of the US-Israel war with Iran, including during a strike in Iran that destroyed a girls' school, killing more than 150 people. 

Amodei concedes "terrible" mistakes will be made but defends the integration of AI in defense by emphasizing that "a human makes the final call."

sample.title: Inside Indonesia's Market Meltdown
sample.description:
Indonesia was once seen as a darling of investors. At $1.5 trillion, Indonesia’s GDP is more than that of Singapore and Thailand combined. Until recently, Indonesia’s stock exchange was the biggest in the region. 

But fears of corruption and policy missteps in markets and government under President Prabowo Subianto are raising concern about the country’s economic future.

sample.description:

sample.title: Why KFC Has Fallen Behind In The U.S.
sample.description:
Chains like Chick-fil-A and Raising Cane's have become fast food mainstays as Americans continue to crave chicken. Yet KFC has become the fourth-largest U.S. chicken chain by market share despite the company saying that ‘it made chicken famous.’ The U.S. has also slipped to its third-largest market behind China and Europe. Now it's rebranding its logos, menu and restaurants as it hopes to reinvigorate sales and cut through the crowded fast-food sector.  CNBC’s Amelia Lucas has more on the company’s innovations.

Produced Shot and Edited by: Natalie Rice
Reporting by: Amelia Lucas
Senior Managing Producer: Tala Hadavi
Additional Footage: Getty Images, KFC

sample.title: What To Expect From Trump’s Trip To The G7 Summit In France
sample.description:
President Trump is set to travel to France for the upcoming G7 summit, which will take place from June 15-17. Markets will be watching for signs of stability as the summit takes place against the backdrop of the Iran war, the Russia-Ukraine war and tensions between the U.S. and Europe over tariffs. CNBC’s Megan Cassella breaks down what to expect from the summit.

Reporter: Megan Cassella
Produced by: Macklin Fishman, Ashlee Trujillo
Edited by: Andrea Miller
Senior Managing Producer: Shawn Baldwin

sample.title: The Next Phase Of The Obesity Drug Race
sample.description:
Five years after Wegovy’s FDA approval, the obesity drug market has evolved rapidly. Novo Nordisk and Eli Lilly introduced GLP-1 pills this year and others like Structure Therapeutics and Zealand Pharma are trying to fight for market share.  But the space is getting crowded. CNBC’s Angelica Peebles has the story from the American Diabetic Association’s conference in New Orleans.

00:38 - 03:10 Mounting competition 
03:11 - 5:38 Next steps in innovation

sample.title: Big Challenges Ahead For Meta AI Chief Alexandr Wang After A Rocky First Year
sample.description:
A year after Meta's $14.3 billion bet on Alexandr Wang, the company finally has its first proprietary AI model, but remains behind OpenAI, Anthropic and Google. High-profile hires from rival labs made headlines, but so did layoffs, key departures, and a year of widespread internal conflict and low morale. With Meta's stock down 19%, the pressure is now on Wang and Zuckerberg to prove the bet was worth it. CNBC’s Julia Boorstin explores what Meta is up against as it attempts to generate real revenue beyond ads.

Reporter: Julia Boorstin
Produced by: Stephen Desaulniers
Edited by: Andrew Evers
Animations: Jason Reginato
Senior Director of Video: Jeniece Pettitt
Additional Footage: Getty Images, Meta

sample.title: How SpaceX’s IPO Is Testing Retail Investors
sample.description:
SpaceX is expected to go public at one of the richest IPO valuations in market history, with retail investors set to receive unusual access through platforms like Robinhood and Charles Schwab. CNBC spoke with five retail traders and investors about why they requested SpaceX IPO shares despite concerns over valuation, volatility, Elon Musk, Starlink, AI infrastructure, launch services and whether public-market buyers could become exit liquidity for early investors.

sample.title: CFTC Chair Michael Selig tackles perpetual futures ‘misconceptions’
sample.description: Michael Selig, Commodity Futures Trading Commission Chair, joins 'Fast Money' to talk perpetual futures, the rise of prediction markets, and more.

sample.title: We will be living and working on the moon in the next five years, says Voyager Technologies CEO
sample.description: Dylan Taylor, Voyager Technologies CEO, joins 'Closing Bell Overtime' to talk investments in space, the SpaceX IPO, and what the future of private space exploration will look like.

sample.title: ETF Edge: Bitcoin kicks off the week with gains as ‘crypto winter’ looks to thaw
sample.description: There are several factors leading investors to have growing confidence in cryptocurrencies. Experts say more traders are coming off the sidelines and investing in the assets, especially now that more products are entering the marketplace. TMX VettaFi head of research and editorial Todd Rosenbluth and CoinDesk president of indices and data David LaValle join Dominic Chu on CNBC’s “ETF Edge” to discuss all this and more.

sample.title: We’re still in ‘early innings’ of bitcoin-related ETPs, CoinDesk’s LaValle says
sample.description: Many investors remain on the sidelines when it comes to cryptocurrencies, but with new products entering the marketplace that may be changing. CoinDesk president of indices and data David LaValle and TMX VettaFi head of research and editorial Todd Rosenbluth sit down with CNBC’s Dominic Chu on “ETF Edge” to break this all down.

sample.title: Bank of Japan raises interest rates to 31-year high
sample.description:
The Bank of Japan raised interest rates to 1% from 0.75%, the highest in three decades, as it moves to tame inflation driven by the energy shock from the Iran war.

sample.description:
Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy and U.S. President Donald Trump joined European leaders for a working group meeting at the G7 Summit in France. The gathering brings together the leaders of France, Britain, Canada, Germany, Italy, Japan and the United States, alongside the European Union.

sample.title: Will the EU let Ukraine in?
sample.description:
Ukraine has opened EU membership talks and is racing through reforms. But full entry still depends on years of negotiations. How long will Ukraine have to wait to join the bloc? Peter Devlin takes a look.

sample.title: She started swinging for her husband. It left her traumatised. #Swinging #BBCNews
She started swinging for her husband. It left her traumatised | BBC News
sample.description:
Ruth says she reluctantly joined a swinging website after she was persuaded by her husband.

It escalated, and she says she had sex with strangers more than 100 times through the website.

For eight months, the BBC has been investigating swinging in the UK, including the role of the FabSwingers website, which Ruth says “facilitated the abuse”.

FabSwingers told the BBC it ran its website in adherence with the regulator Ofcom’s requirements.

Details of organisations offering information about or support after sexual abuse or with feelings of despair are available at bbc.co.uk/actionline

Listen to the full podcast 'Swingers' here: https://www.bbc.co.uk/sounds/brand/p0nqywd0

Subscribe to our channel here: https://bbc.in/bbcnews

For the latest news download the BBC News app or visit BBC.com/news
